automotive-ev-battery.title: ProLogium targets SPAC merger to fund French battery plant
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Taiwanese solid-state battery manufacturer ProLogium announced plans to merge with shell company Translational Development Acquisition Corp. to secure financing for its battery factory under construction in Dunkirk, France. This transaction represents a significant development in solid-state battery manufacturing in Europe, a critical technology for next-generation electric vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: CATL launches battery-swapping network for light electric trucks in southern China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: CATL and logistics provider DST launched the first standardized battery swap system for light electric trucks, targeting the Greater Bay Area with plans to expand from 31 to 140 swap stations by end of 2026. This development addresses a key infrastructure challenge for commercial EV adoption in China's logistics sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Over 200,000 EV Public Charging Points In Germany Now
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Germany has surpassed 200,000 public EV charging points, with over 149,000 slow chargers and 51,000 fast chargers installed across the country. This milestone demonstrates Germany's substantial progress in EV infrastructure development, positioning it as a leader in European charging infrastructure expansion.

automotive-ev-battery.title: UTA Edenred launches Ionity subscriptions for fleet customers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mobility service provider UTA Edenred partnered with Ionity to offer discounted charging subscriptions at 6,500 charging points across Europe. This collaboration with the major charging network operator expands fleet charging options and represents growing industry consolidation around key charging infrastructure players.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New BYD Yuan Plus Hits Global Automotive Sweet Spot. When Will It Hit Global Markets?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD's new Yuan Plus, featuring a rear-wheel drive platform and advanced 5-minute 10-70 percent flash charging capability, has launched in China as the successor to the globally-sold ATTO 3. The vehicle's specifications and pricing suggest significant competitive potential for BYD's expansion into international markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Latin America reaches 10,000 electric buses in operation
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Latin America and the Caribbean have reached a milestone of over 10,000 electric buses in operation, with the majority sourced from Chinese manufacturers. The region continues to experience strong growth in electric bus adoption, particularly in major metropolitan areas.

automotive-ev-battery.title: USA: PositivEnergy expands EV charging network in Philadelphia
automotive-ev-battery.summary: PositivEnergy expanded its partnership with Philadelphia to deploy approximately 435 public EV charging ports, including DC fast chargers and Level 2 charging points. This expansion supports the city's electrification goals while serving residents, commuters, and visitors.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chery to Enter Japan Through an Autobacs Partnership
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker Chery's subsidiary Electric Mobility Technologies plans to enter Japan's kei-car market with a compact EV launch scheduled for 2027 under the EMTA brand. This entry represents intensifying competition from Chinese EV manufacturers in Japan's traditionally domestic-focused market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW recalls new iX3 over electric shock risk
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW initiated a recall of its new iX3 flagship model due to technical issues that could potentially expose users to electric shock when touching the vehicle body, though the issue currently affects only a small number of vehicles. The recall highlights quality assurance challenges in the development of new EV platforms.

automotive-ev-battery.title: First drive in the Cupra Raval VZ: Why the small spaniard delivers more than just looks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Cupra Raval VZ, positioned as the sportier alternative to the Volkswagen ID. Polo, demonstrated strong handling dynamics during initial testing in Barcelona, though the performance-focused approach involves design and comfort compromises. The vehicle underscores Cupra's strategic emphasis on emotional driving experiences within the compact electric vehicle segment.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers a black hole that formed before its host galaxy
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed ancient galaxies known as "Little Red Dot" that contain black holes appearing to have formed before their host galaxies, challenging conventional astronomical theory. This discovery could represent a fundamental paradigm shift in understanding the relationship between black holes and galaxy formation.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Mission Preps to Unveil New Populations of Faraway Worlds
science-space.summary: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is positioned to dramatically accelerate exoplanet discovery by identifying approximately 100,000 worlds beyond our solar system. This represents a tenfold increase compared to the roughly 6,300 exoplanets currently known from NASA and partner missions.

science-space.title: NASA's X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
science-space.summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is preparing for a critical series of test flights, including its first mission at supersonic speeds as part of the Quesst program. These flights will validate the aircraft's ability to achieve mission-critical objectives in high-speed flight conditions.

science-space.title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
science-space.summary: Analysis of 20 samples collected by NASA's Curiosity Rover has identified hematite crystallite size variations at different elevations as a new mineralogical marker for studying Mars' ancient climate. This discovery provides a novel tool for interpreting geological evidence of past environmental conditions on the Red Planet.

science-space.title: Most powerful 'ghost particle' ever may have come from a cosmic particle accelerator fed by a black hole
science-space.summary: The most energetic neutrino ever detected may have originated from blazars powered by black holes, suggesting these cosmic phenomena function as extraordinarily powerful particle accelerators. The discovery advances understanding of extreme astrophysical events and high-energy particle production in the universe.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has successfully completed a drill sample extraction at the "Campo Marte" location on Mars, marking a significant milestone in ongoing geological investigations. The recovered sample will provide crucial data for understanding Martian geology and potential evidence of past habitability.

science-space.title: What's next for SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after its historic debut flight?
science-space.summary: SpaceX's next-generation Starship V3 megarocket completed its inaugural flight successfully, demonstrating the vehicle's capabilities and establishing a foundation for future missions. The company is now planning subsequent test flights and operational objectives for the powerful launch system.

science-space.title: NASA is hatching a 'fast-paced plan' to boost this space telescope. But first, they'll have to find it
science-space.summary: NASA is developing a plan to have a private spacecraft rendezvous with and boost the altitude of its Swift space telescope to extend its operational lifespan. The agency is currently calculating the observatory's orbital trajectory to enable the precise rendezvous needed for the altitude adjustment.

science-space.title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
science-space.summary: NASA aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs discusses challenges facing the agency's Artemis lunar landing systems, particularly dust and visibility issues during descent to the Moon's surface. His insights address engineering solutions being developed to overcome environmental obstacles to safe human landings.

science-space.title: New Landsat Science Team Holds First In-Person Meeting
science-space.summary: The new Landsat Science Team convened for its inaugural in-person meeting at the Earth Resources Observation and Science Center in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, from May 5-7. The gathering officially introduced team members serving the 2026-2030 cycle for Landsat 8, 9, and 10 Earth observation missions.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H funding round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, positioning the AI startup near unicorn status ahead of an anticipated initial public offering. The massive raise underscores continued investor confidence in large language model development and suggests this may be Anthropic's final private fundraising before going public.

tech-ai.title: The internet is being rebuilt for machines
tech-ai.summary: Major cloud infrastructure providers including AWS and Cloudflare are redesigning their platforms to accommodate AI agents moving from experimental phases into production environments. This structural shift reflects a fundamental change in internet traffic patterns, with machine-generated requests increasingly replacing traditional human-user interactions.

tech-ai.title: Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind
tech-ai.summary: Waymo has significantly outpaced competitors in autonomous vehicle registrations, according to newly available tracking data and regulatory filings in Texas. The data provides the most comprehensive accounting to date of robotaxis and self-driving trucks actively deployed on roads, with Waymo's lead underscoring its market position in the autonomous vehicle sector.

tech-ai.title: Claude's new model is more 'honest' when it messes up
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic is releasing Claude Opus 4.8, which the company claims demonstrates improved honesty by flagging uncertainty rather than confidently presenting unsupported conclusions. The update addresses a common AI model limitation where systems jump to conclusions and overstate confidence in thin evidence.

tech-ai.title: Glean's top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling point
tech-ai.summary: Enterprise AI search startup Glean has tripled its annual revenue to over $300 million, driven largely by cost-cutting benefits that appeal to enterprises facing AI budget pressures. The company's growth demonstrates market demand for efficiency-focused AI solutions even as major tech giants expand their offerings in the category.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot gets a speed boost and cleaner design
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft has launched a redesigned version of Microsoft 365 Copilot featuring a cleaner interface that loads twice as fast and delivers more structured, scannable responses. The update includes a "progressive disclosure" feature that contextually presents tools based on user prompts, rolling out across desktop and mobile platforms.

tech-ai.title: Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI
tech-ai.summary: Asana has acquired StackAI, a no-code platform for building AI agents, integrating it into its expanding suite of AI workflow tools. The acquisition strengthens Asana's position in the AI-powered productivity space and lowers barriers for non-technical users to create automated workflows.

tech-ai.title: Hackers are trying to steal Signal users' backups in new wave of phishing attacks
tech-ai.summary: A new phishing campaign is targeting Signal users by attempting to trick them into surrendering their secret recovery keys, which can unlock encrypted message backups. The coordinated attack represents an escalation in efforts to compromise end-to-end encrypted messaging platforms.

tech-ai.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida
tech-ai.summary: Blue Origin experienced a significant setback as its New Glenn rocket exploded during testing in Florida, complicating the company's competitive efforts against SpaceX. The incident is likely to impact the spaceflight company's timeline and development roadmap as it works to compete in the commercial launch market.

tech-ai.title: YouTube takes baby steps to being a real podcast app
tech-ai.summary: YouTube is adding podcast-focused features for Premium subscribers, including a new "on-the-go mode" that shifts to audio-first playback with simplified controls and episode chapter timelines. The incremental update suggests YouTube's growing effort to capture market share in the audio streaming and podcast consumption space.

world-news.title: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
world-news.summary: US officials report that a framework for a ceasefire extension deal between the United States and Iran has been agreed in principle, though final approval from Trump and Iran's leadership remains pending. The development marks significant progress in diplomatic negotiations amid heightened tensions in the Middle East.

world-news.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli army to seize 70 percent of Gaza
world-news.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has issued orders for the military to take control of 70 percent of Gaza, escalating the territorial posture in the ongoing conflict. This directive represents a significant shift in Israel's military strategy and has major implications for the humanitarian situation and regional stability.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran, Trump yet to comment on 60-day truce extension plan
world-news.summary: A 60-day truce extension plan between Iran and the United States awaits comment from both Tehran and the Trump administration, while Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam criticizes Israel's military operations in Lebanon. The developments underscore ongoing diplomatic uncertainty in regional conflicts.

world-news.title: Israel hits Lebanese capital in 'targeted strike'
world-news.summary: Israel conducted a targeted military strike on Beirut, marking an escalation as the Lebanese capital had previously been spared amid the broader conflict with Hezbollah. Both sides have accused each other of violating a ceasefire agreement reached last month.

world-news.title: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
world-news.summary: Hezbollah has adopted fibre-optic drone technology as its primary weapon system against Israeli military and civilian targets, drawing tactics learned from the Ukraine conflict. The development represents a significant technological escalation in the Israel-Hezbollah conflict.

world-news.title: Guatemala denies agreeing to US strikes against drug traffickers
world-news.summary: Guatemala's President Bernardo Arevalo's government has denied approving US military strikes against drug traffickers, claiming it only requested security cooperation rather than direct military action. The dispute highlights tensions between the United States and Central American nations over regional security policy.

world-news.title: US to designate two Brazilian gangs as 'terrorist' organisations
world-news.summary: The Trump administration plans to designate two Brazilian criminal gangs as terrorist organizations, using crime and drug trafficking concerns to advance broader US military and geopolitical interests across Latin America. The move signals an escalation in US intervention in regional security matters.

world-news.title: Colombia's first left-wing gov't has reduced poverty, but has pile of debt
world-news.summary: Colombia's first left-wing government has achieved poverty reduction but faces substantial debt challenges that threaten long-term economic sustainability and social progress. The incoming administration must balance debt reduction with preserving social gains achieved during the current term.

world-news.title: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
world-news.summary: Israeli military strikes on Gaza City have killed at least five children in an operation reportedly targeting a Hamas military commander. The incident highlights the ongoing civilian toll in the Gaza conflict.

world-news.title: Are US and Iran close to peace or sliding back to war?
world-news.summary: Despite recent military exchanges between the United States and Iran, neither side appears interested in escalating to full-scale conflict, leaving the trajectory of negotiations uncertain. The competing signals suggest diplomatic possibilities remain open despite heightened tensions.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic finalises $65bn funding deal to surpass OpenAI's valuation
finance-markets.summary: Anthropic has completed a $65 billion funding round that values the AI company at $965 billion, surpassing OpenAI's current valuation and establishing it as one of the most valuable private companies globally. The funding demonstrates the ongoing investor appetite for artificial intelligence ventures and marks a significant milestone in the competitive AI development landscape.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX Lowers IPO Valuation Target to at Least $1.8 Trillion
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX has reduced its initial public offering valuation target to at least $1.8 trillion as Elon Musk's rocket and AI company approaches its market debut. The adjustment reflects market conditions while still positioning the company as one of the most valuable entities entering public markets.

finance-markets.title: US, Iran Agree to Truce Renewal Pending Trump Signoff
finance-markets.summary: The United States and Iran have reached a tentative agreement to extend their ceasefire by 60 days and launch further discussions on Tehran's nuclear program, pending Trump administration approval. The deal raises optimism that the three-month Middle East conflict may be approaching resolution with significant implications for regional stability and global energy markets.

finance-markets.title: Oil Set for Worst Month Since 2020 as US, Iran Near Longer Truce
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices have declined sharply, with Brent crude set for its largest monthly drop since 2020 as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend their ceasefire by 60 days, raising expectations that energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz may resume. The ceasefire optimism has eased supply concerns that previously supported higher commodity prices.

finance-markets.title: America's hegemonic glory is under threat
finance-markets.summary: Long-term US Treasury yields are rising as investors reassess American economic dominance, driven by concerns over excessive government spending on defense and social programs combined with structural fiscal pressures. The trend raises questions about the sustainability of US debt levels and the dollar's long-term reserve currency status.

finance-markets.title: Japanese Banks Stoke Hybrid Bond Boom to Fund Regulatory Capital
finance-markets.summary: Japan's major banks are issuing hybrid bonds at their fastest pace in over a decade to meet higher regulatory capital requirements under evolving financial standards. The surge reflects banks' efforts to strengthen balance sheets while adapting to stricter regulatory frameworks.

finance-markets.title: Dollar's Monthly Rise Leaves Strategists Wary of Further Gains
finance-markets.summary: The dollar rallied significantly this month as traders priced in expectations of higher US interest rates, but Wall Street strategists are now cautious about continued appreciation. The hesitation reflects concerns about currency valuation levels and potential headwinds to further dollar strength.

finance-markets.title: Ambani Says Reliance Evaluating 'Strategic Pathways' for Jio, No Word on IPO
finance-markets.summary: Reliance Industries is taking deliberate steps to strengthen governance at its Jio Platforms digital unit ahead of a planned public listing expected to value the subsidiary at as much as $4 billion. The company is exploring strategic options for the unit while carefully managing its path to the market.

finance-markets.title: Musk's tweet undermines SpaceX's claims about Anthropic data centre deal
finance-markets.summary: Elon Musk has contradicted SpaceX's IPO filing claims regarding the company's data centre arrangement with Anthropic, stating the deal lasts only 180 days rather than the three-year term described in regulatory documents. The discrepancy raises questions about SpaceX's disclosure accuracy and the true duration of its AI infrastructure commitments.

finance-markets.title: EU wants crisis powers to seize control of chip supplies
finance-markets.summary: The European Union is drafting legislation that would grant authorities emergency powers to force chipmakers to override existing contracts and prioritize EU supply chains during crises. The measure reflects Europe's strategic concerns about semiconductor supply security and technological self-sufficiency amid global geopolitical tensions.

health-biotech.title: GSK spells out positive Phase 3 data for hepatitis B drug it licensed from Ionis
health-biotech.summary: GlaxoSmithKline has released detailed results from Phase 3 trials of a chronic hepatitis B treatment licensed from Ionis Pharmaceuticals, with regulatory reviews currently underway in four markets including the United States. The positive data represents a significant advancement in treating a disease affecting millions globally and demonstrates the commercial viability of the partnership.

health-biotech.title: AbbVie clinches FDA approval for ultra-rare blood cancer ADC from ImmunoGen purchase
health-biotech.summary: AbbVie has received FDA approval for an antibody-drug conjugate targeting an ultra-rare but aggressive form of blood cancer, acquired through its $10.1 billion acquisition of ImmunoGen. This regulatory milestone expands AbbVie's oncology portfolio and validates the strategic acquisition of the immunotherapy company.

health-biotech.title: Lilly obesity meds Foundayo and Zepbound to gain CVS coverage
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly's flagship obesity medications Foundayo and Zepbound will be included in CVS Caremark formularies, significantly expanding access in the highly competitive commercial drug market. This formulary decision is expected to substantially boost sales of the company's GLP-1 therapies.

health-biotech.title: Blood pressure tech floods the market after FDA relaxes wearables oversight
health-biotech.summary: Following the FDA's relaxation of oversight standards for wellness devices, numerous wearables with unvalidated blood pressure measurement technology have entered the market, raising concerns about accuracy and consumer safety. This regulatory shift has created a surge in commercial blood pressure monitoring products without rigorous clinical validation.

health-biotech.title: China gene editing startup YolTech raises $70M to move CRISPR therapies forward
health-biotech.summary: YolTech, one of China's leading gene editing companies, has secured its largest funding round to advance CRISPR-based therapeutic development with plans to pursue a public listing in Hong Kong. The investment reflects growing momentum in Asian gene therapy innovation despite recent consolidation among U.S. competitors.

health-biotech.title: Oura pushes deeper into healthcare services as part of new ring launch
health-biotech.summary: Oura has unveiled its new Ring 5 alongside plans to integrate health records and translate ring data into actionable medical care, advancing the wearables company's transition into clinical healthcare services. This expansion positions Oura as a bridge between consumer health monitoring and professional medical intervention.

health-biotech.title: Charles River CEO says it likely won't be the last manufacturer to break away from cell and gene therapy
health-biotech.summary: Charles River Laboratories CEO Birgit Girshick has indicated that other contract manufacturers are likely to follow the company's recent decision to exit cell and gene therapy manufacturing. This trend suggests a broader industry shift in manufacturing consolidation and service model restructuring within the advanced therapy sector.

health-biotech.title: Niowave's $75M radioisotope facility; CordenPharma to buy AmbioPharm
health-biotech.summary: Niowave has broken ground on a $75 million radioisotope manufacturing facility in Lansing, Michigan to produce medical isotopes for diagnostic and therapeutic applications. The facility expansion reflects growing demand for reliable domestic radioisotope supply for nuclear medicine.

health-biotech.title: Biotech veteran Jeremy Levin on why the industry's future is secure, but American leadership is at risk
health-biotech.summary: Biotech executive Jeremy Levin has highlighted that while the industry continues to produce unprecedented scientific breakthroughs, American institutional support—including regulatory, investment, and public infrastructure—faces competitive pressures that could undermine U.S. leadership. The commentary underscores broader concerns about maintaining American dominance in biotechnology innovation.

health-biotech.title: As Ebola outbreak in Central Africa grows, the U.S. turns itself into a fortress
health-biotech.summary: The United States is implementing extraordinary measures to prevent Ebola entry, though public health experts warn such policies may inadvertently increase risks for Americans infected abroad and discourage critical volunteer deployment to outbreak regions. The restrictive approach reflects heightened disease containment concerns amid expanding Central African transmission.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and classified agency secrets on a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional inquiries from both houses. CISA continues efforts to contain the breach and invalidate the compromised credentials, raising serious questions about federal cybersecurity infrastructure security.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited known vulnerabilities in older Internet routers to harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users across more than 18,000 networks. The sophisticated campaign allowed state-backed actors to siphon credentials without deploying malware, representing a significant supply-chain style attack vector.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch authorities arrested two co-owners of hosting companies operating infrastructure used by Russia to conduct cyberattacks, influence operations, and disinformation campaigns targeting the European Union. The 800 seized servers had been used by sanctioned entities to facilitate attacks across EU member states.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released fixes for 167 security vulnerabilities including a SharePoint Server zero-day and the publicly disclosed Windows Defender flaw "BlueHammer." Additionally, Google Chrome patched its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe released emergency updates addressing an actively exploited Reader vulnerability allowing remote code execution.

cybersecurity.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
cybersecurity.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor group deployed sophisticated social engineering campaigns targeting cryptocurrency organizations using recruitment-themed lures and custom macOS malware. The attacks included deep targeting of CI/CD infrastructure to facilitate digital asset theft.

cybersecurity.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors continue exploiting a patched critical vulnerability in FortiClient Endpoint Management Server to deliver credential-stealing malware across managed endpoints. The campaign abuses trusted endpoint management infrastructure, demonstrating how attackers weaponize enterprise security tools.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for conducting text-message phishing attacks. The member's attacks compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in tens of millions of dollars in stolen cryptocurrency.

cybersecurity.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
cybersecurity.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Gogs, a popular open-source Git service, allows any authenticated user to execute arbitrary code with a CVSS score of 9.4. The flaw poses significant risk to organizations using self-hosted Gogs deployments.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian security firm specializing in DDoS protection was found enabling a botnet responsible for massive attacks against other Brazilian network operators. The firm's executive attributed the malicious activity to a security breach, though the incident raises questions about insider threats and competitive sabotage in the security industry.

cybersecurity.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
cybersecurity.summary: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating throughout Brazil and Latin America using a Malware-as-a-Service licensing model with a no-code development interface. The accessibility and distribution method indicate potentially widespread adoption among threat actors in the region.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid's pre-IPO SpaceX contracts suffers 45% flash crash, liquidating $1.5 million
crypto-web3.summary: A severe selloff in a SpaceX-linked crypto token on Hyperliquid resulted in a 45% price collapse within 30 minutes, triggering $1.5 million in liquidations across hundreds of retail traders. The incident highlights systemic liquidity risks in emerging decentralized finance platforms where insufficient market depth can amplify volatility.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto trading firm FalconX confidentially files with SEC for IPO, hires bankers
crypto-web3.summary: FalconX, a major cryptocurrency trading firm, has filed confidential draft registration statements with the SEC and engaged Cantor Fitzgerald and other banking advisors for an anticipated initial public offering. The listing is expected later in 2026 as market conditions stabilize.

crypto-web3.title: Sui blockchain suffers another network outage as transactions grind to a halt
crypto-web3.summary: The Sui blockchain experienced its second network outage of the year, causing transaction processing to halt entirely. The recurring outages raise concerns about the platform's operational reliability and infrastructure resilience.

crypto-web3.title: UniCredit warns Europe may struggle to contain crypto-bank crisis under MiCA rules
crypto-web3.summary: UniCredit has cautioned that European Union deposit insurance protections, capped at €100,000 per account, may prove insufficient to manage potential systemic stress from large stablecoin reserve holdings under the Markets in Crypto Assets (MiCA) regulatory framework. This contrasts with broader U.S. regulatory safeguards for similar holdings.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi's turn: Prediction markets platform sues Minnesota over law going into effect in August
crypto-web3.summary: Prediction markets platform Kalshi has filed a lawsuit against Minnesota following Governor Tim Walz's signing of legislation that prohibits prediction market operations within the state. The suit comes as the CFTC simultaneously challenges similar restrictions, signaling an escalating regulatory conflict.

crypto-web3.title: Tether's U.S.-focused stablecoin grows over 500% in a month, but still lags main rivals
crypto-web3.summary: Tether's USAT stablecoin achieved explosive 500% monthly growth with market capitalization surpassing $140 million in April. However, the token remains significantly behind established competitors including Circle's USDC, PayPal's PYUSD, and Ripple's RLUSD in terms of total adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Bitwise bets Hyperliquid could power future finance as HYPE ETFs gain traction
crypto-web3.summary: Bitwise is positioning the Hyperliquid blockchain as foundational infrastructure for decentralized finance, citing strong investor demand for HYPE token exposure through newly launched exchange-traded funds. The firm's conviction reflects broader institutional interest in decentralized trading platforms.

crypto-web3.title: The Digital Chamber ramps up pressure on Congress with call to action to pass Clarity Act
crypto-web3.summary: The Digital Chamber has launched a digital advocacy platform to mobilize constituent communication with lawmakers, intensifying lobbying efforts for passage of the Clarity Act. The initiative represents a coordinated push for comprehensive U.S. cryptocurrency regulatory legislation.

crypto-web3.title: Calamos bets protected Bitcoin ETFs can outlast crypto market swings
crypto-web3.summary: Asset manager Calamos is promoting Bitcoin exchange-traded funds with embedded downside protection mechanisms as investors rotate away from traditional spot Bitcoin products amid recent market volatility. More than $1 billion has exited spot Bitcoin ETFs in recent weeks.

crypto-web3.title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin whale and dolphin accumulation stalled as demand remains weak
crypto-web3.summary: CryptoQuant analysis indicates that large Bitcoin holder accumulation has plateaued, with whale and dolphin balance growth stalling amid subdued market demand. The data suggests deteriorating holding structure concentration among institutional and sophisticated investors.

startups-vc.title: Glean’s top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling point
startups-vc.summary: The enterprise AI search startup tripled its annual revenue even as tech giants entered the category.

startups-vc.title: How to apply to Startup Battlefield 2026, what you need ahead of the June 8 deadline
startups-vc.summary: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

startups-vc.title: Bluesky embraces long-form content to counter X Articles
startups-vc.summary: In its latest update, Bluesky is getting into long-form content.

startups-vc.title: H1 secures $40M from CVS, proving SaaS startups can still attract investment
startups-vc.summary: CEO Ariel Katz argues that while AI can replicate workflow SaaS, it can't copy H1’s unique doctor data.

startups-vc.title: Why Paris may be the most important AI city outside Silicon Valley
startups-vc.summary: Europe’s startup ecosystem has matured significantly; its founders are increasingly willing to scale companies domestically instead of immediately looking to relocate to the U.S.

startups-vc.title: In just 3 weeks, StrictlyVC is coming to Los Angeles
startups-vc.summary: StrictlyVC Los Angeles is on June 18. Join for meaningful networking and fireside chats with leaders from Mach Industries, Shinkei Systems, and more. Register today.

startups-vc.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
startups-vc.summary: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

startups-vc.title: Corgi announces $106M raise at $2.6B valuation — double what it was worth 3 weeks ago
startups-vc.summary: While startups raising back-to-back rounds at steep step-ups have become almost routine, a company whose valuation doubles in three weeks is unusual enough to raise questions, particularly given the investor set in both rounds is the same.

startups-vc.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Databricks’ co-founder on what kills enterprise AI deals
startups-vc.summary: Enterprise AI is entering a different phase now, one where enterprises are no longer evaluating whether AI is exciting. They are evaluating whether it is safe to deploy broadly.

startups-vc.title: 2 days left: Lock in ticket savings of up to $410 to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
startups-vc.summary: Savings of up to $410 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets end tomorrow, May 29, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now to save and join 10,000+ tech leaders on October 13-15 in San Francisco.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: China's new carbon metric leaves Germany-sized gap in its emissions
energy-climate.summary: China has fundamentally altered how it measures its core climate goal, effectively creating a significant measurement gap equivalent to Germany's total emissions. This change has major implications for global climate accountability and China's reported progress toward its carbon intensity targets.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 22 May 2026: UN adopts landmark resolution | Trump takes on 'RCP8.5' | Climate migration
energy-climate.summary: The UN adopted a landmark climate resolution this week while new political challenges emerged regarding climate science interpretation and climate-driven migration. The digest covers major international climate policy developments and their potential consequences.

energy-climate.title: Media reaction: UK and Europe's 'mind-boggling' May heat and climate change
energy-climate.summary: Europe experienced a severe heatwave in May that shattered temperature records across multiple countries, with media outlets highlighting the connection to climate change. The event underscores the accelerating impacts of global warming on the continent.

energy-climate.title: Critical minerals are becoming the energy transition's delivery constraint
energy-climate.summary: The renewable energy sector is entering a more challenging industrial phase where critical mineral availability is emerging as a significant constraint on deployment and expansion. This supply-side challenge threatens to slow the pace of the global energy transition.

energy-climate.title: U.S. International Trade Commission extends tariffs on Chinese and Taiwanese solar imports
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. International Trade Commission has extended antidumping and countervailing duties on solar imports from China and Taiwan following a sunset review. The decision maintains trade barriers that impact solar sector competitiveness and renewable energy deployment costs.

energy-climate.title: China Briefing 28 May 2026: Deadly rains | China pushes back | Examining China's carbon intensity metric
energy-climate.summary: China's briefing covers severe weather events, policy responses, and examination of China's revised carbon intensity measurement methodology. The coverage provides context on China's climate challenges and evolving carbon accounting practices.

energy-climate.title: Sungrow to supply 7.5GWh of storage for Masdar's Abu Dhabi RTC project
energy-climate.summary: Sungrow has secured an agreement to supply energy storage systems and photovoltaic inverter technologies for Masdar's gigascale renewable energy project in Abu Dhabi. The deal represents significant progress in large-scale renewable and storage infrastructure development in the Middle East.

energy-climate.title: Swiss power distributors allowed to remunerate solar power at market rates
energy-climate.summary: Switzerland will permit grid operators to compensate photovoltaic electricity at real-time market prices beginning in 2027, while protecting small PV systems with minimum remuneration premiums. The policy reform aims to better align solar generation with electricity demand patterns.

energy-climate.title: Revealed: Floods have forced at least 67 closures at NHS hospitals since 2021
energy-climate.summary: At least 67 NHS hospital wards and departments across the UK have been forced to close due to flooding since 2021, highlighting climate change impacts on critical healthcare infrastructure. The findings demonstrate the tangible effects of extreme weather on essential services.

energy-climate.title: Argentina storage tender attracts 235 bids totaling over 8,300 MW
energy-climate.summary: Argentina's battery storage tender received 235 bids representing over 8,300 megawatts of capacity, demonstrating strong market interest in energy storage development. The successful tender process indicates growing momentum in Latin America's renewable energy infrastructure buildout.

asia-pacific.title: Note for Trump: US Treasury designs US$250 bill with president’s face
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump could soon appear on a new US$250 bill, in the Republican’s latest move to shatter US traditions by putting his personal stamp on national institutions.
A proposal for the new bill, featuring a glaring Trump, was first reported Thursday by The Washington Post.
If carried out, it would be the first time the image of a living person - let alone a president - had appeared on US currency in a century and a half.
“Right now there is proposed legislation - front of the House,

asia-pacific.title: Is self-censorship behind Japan’s ‘problematic’ press freedom ranking?
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan is often seen as one of Asia’s most stable democracies, with a sophisticated media industry and constitutional protections for free expression, yet one of the world’s leading press freedom indexes has once again rated the country’s media environment as “problematic”.
Analysts and journalists said the label points to a contradiction at the heart of Japan’s press system – reporters are rarely subject to the overt repression seen in authoritarian states, but political pressure, access...

asia-pacific.title: TV coverage of Trump’s visit showed Americans how ordinary Chinese live
asia-pacific.summary:
Nothing about the summit between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump in Beijing suggested high drama. The language was restrained, the optics disciplined. The impression it left was simple: escalation of tensions for its own sake is a losing strategy.
The announcement that China would buy 200 Boeing aircraft served mainly as a political gesture of goodwill, with the commercial details to be filled in later by negotiators. More important was the pattern across readouts from

asia-pacific.title: ‘Save her first’: China expectant mum relinquishes surgery room slot to bleeding pregnant woman
asia-pacific.summary:
A pregnant woman in eastern China gave up her prepared operating room to save another expectant mother suffering massive bleeding.
A video of the emergency “life-and-death relay” in Weifang, Shandong province, has gone viral on mainland social media.
According to People’s Daily, the story unfolded at Weifang People’s Hospital, where an unnamed expectant mother was waiting in the operating room fully prepared for surgery.
She had fasted for nearly two hours before the procedure while her unborn..

asia-pacific.title: Death of Hongkong Post? Mounting losses, dwindling demand cast shadow over service
asia-pacific.summary:
In Hong Kong’s busy commercial area of Causeway Bay, a post office located in a major shopping centre served just a few dozen customers during lunch on Thursday.
Across 90 minutes, when nearby restaurants and shops were filled with residents pressed for time, the post office stayed mostly empty, its workers outnumbering the people they were serving.
Hongkong Post is struggling with the same existential threat facing other traditional mail service operators worldwide: how to stay relevant in a...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Transitory euphoria’: South Korea’s strong economic outlook masks key hurdles
asia-pacific.summary:
South Korea’s economy faces uncertainties from rising inflation, a weakening won and uneven performance across sectors, even as it grows stronger than expected this year, according to analysts.
The Bank of Korea on Thursday revised its economic growth outlook for this year to 2.6 per cent, up 0.6 percentage points from its forecast three months ago, citing robust exports driven by booming semiconductor demand and government supplementary spending aimed at offsetting the shocks from the Middle...

asia-pacific.title: Netanyahu directs Israeli forces to expand Gaza control to 70%
asia-pacific.summary:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday he had directed Israel’s military to take more of Gaza, initially by seizing 70 per cent of the Palestinian territory, where the population is already penned into a tiny strip of land along the coast.
Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of the tiny coastal strip, bombarded to ruins by Israel’s two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas ‌attack on southern Israel.
Under an October US-brokered truce that has...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong well placed to help Central Asia grow yuan trade, leading banker says
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong has the “most complete renminbi financial ecosystem” and is well placed to serve as a hub for Central Asian countries seeking to expand the currency’s use in trade and investment, according to a veteran banker.
Stephen Chan Man, deputy chief executive of Bank of China (Hong Kong), also said on Thursday that the high-level delegation led by Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu next month to Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan was an opportunity to deepen financial ties with the region under a...

asia-pacific.title: Premium plan: China’s EV makers pin hopes on high-end models to buttress margins
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese electric vehicle (EV) makers are speeding up their pivot to the premium segment, where they hope to secure higher profit margins and dodge the cutthroat market’s most vicious price competition.
New models launched by carmakers including Nio and Seres also mount a challenge to Tesla’s bestselling Model Y in the world’s largest EV market.
“Intensified efforts to gain ascendancy in the premium segment show the carmakers’ confidence in their technologies,” said Chen Jinzhu, CEO of...

asia-pacific.title: Texas fire official confirms fatalities, injuries in a huge blaze at residential building
asia-pacific.summary:
A huge fire destroyed a two-storey block of flats in Dallas, Texas on Thursday, causing fatalities and injuring at least four people. The search for the missing people is continuing, a fire official said.
The blaze sent huge plumes of black smoke into the sky and drew a massive firefighter response.
“There have been fatalities at this point,” Dallas Fire-Rescue Deputy Chief Mark Berry said at a news conference, adding the mission has changed from rescue to recovery. “Let us work through the...
